question_title: How many executions will be carried out in the United States between June 25 and June 28, 2026 (inclusive)?
summary: This question asks how many US executions will occur June 25-28, 2026. Only one execution is scheduled: Dusty Ray Spencer in Florida on June 25. The distribution is heavily concentrated at 1 (Spencer proceeds, ~84%) with meaningful probability at 0 (Spencer is stayed, ~15%) and negligible probability of 2+ (~2%). Spencer's circuit court postconviction claims were denied, and the execution is marked "serious" by the SMU tracker. However, a Florida Supreme Court appeal (SC2026-0880) remains open with novel arguments about his age (74) and allegedly expired drugs, creating real stay risk. No other executions are scheduled in any US state for this window.

factors.description: Only one execution (Dusty Ray Spencer, FL, June 25) is scheduled in the June 25-28 window per DPIC and SMU tracker. No other US executions scheduled.
Spencer's execution marked 'serious' by SMU tracker; circuit court denied postconviction claims; DeSantis-era FL very aggressive with ~7-8 FL executions already in 2026
Florida Supreme Court appeal SC2026-0880 still open as of June 17. Novel arguments about age (74, would be oldest FL execution) and allegedly expired lethal drugs create real stay risk.
No other executions scheduled anywhere in US for June 26-28. Next confirmed is FL July 14. Emergency scheduling essentially never happens.
factors.conditional: Constrains upper tail - P(2+) very low

reasoning:
**Forecast Summary: US Executions June 25-28, 2026**

The distribution is sharply concentrated on a single outcome:

| Outcome | Probability |
|---------|-------------|
| 0 executions | ~15% |
| 1 execution | ~84% |
| 2 executions | ~1.3% |
| 3+ executions | <0.2% |

**Key findings:**
- **Only one execution is scheduled**: Dusty Ray Spencer in Florida on June 25, 2026 (lethal injection)
- **Most likely outcome is 1** (~84%): Spencer's execution proceeds. His circuit court postconviction claims were denied, the execution is marked "serious" by independent trackers, and Florida has been very active with executions under DeSantis (10 warrants signed in 2026)
- **~15% chance of 0**: The main risk is a stay from the Florida Supreme Court, where Spencer's appeal (SC2026-0880) remains open with novel arguments about his advanced age (74) and allegedly expired lethal drugs
- **P(2+) is negligible**: No other executions are scheduled anywhere in the US for this 4-day window

resolution_criteria: This question asks how many executions of condemned prisoners are carried out (i.e., the prisoner is put to death) in the United States — by any state or the federal government — on the calendar dates June 25, 26, 27, or 28, 2026 (US local time of the executing jurisdiction). An execution counts only if it is actually carried out during the window; a scheduled execution that is stayed, postponed, reprieved, commuted, or otherwise not completed does not count. Report the integer count; for example, if exactly one execution is carried out, the value is 1. Resolution will be based on the Death Penalty Information Center's records of 2026 executions (https://deathpenaltyinfo.org/executions/upcoming-executions and its 2026 execution list). If DPIC's records are unavailable or unclear, official state Department of Corrections statements or [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions) will be used.
fine_print: Only executions of human prisoners under a death sentence count; this excludes deaths from other causes (e.g., natural death, suicide). The relevant date is the date on which the prisoner is pronounced dead, using the local calendar date of the executing jurisdiction. Both lethal-injection and any other method (e.g., nitrogen hypoxia, electrocution) count equally. If an execution scheduled within the window begins before midnight local time but is completed shortly after, it counts toward whichever date the prisoner is pronounced dead.

factors.description: Only one execution (Dusty Ray Spencer, FL, June 25) is scheduled in the June 25-28 window. No other executions are scheduled anywhere in the US for those dates.
Spencer's execution is marked 'serious' by SMU tracker, circuit court denied postconviction claims, DeSantis-era FL has ~70-85% warrant completion rate, ~8-9 FL executions already carried out in 2026
Florida Supreme Court appeal (SC2026-0880) still pending/open as of June 17 - creates some stay risk. Novel arguments about age (74, oldest ever FL execution) and expired drugs
No other executions scheduled anywhere in the US for June 26-28. Next confirmed execution is FL July 14. Emergency scheduling in 4-day window essentially never happens.
factors.conditional: Constrains upper tail - P(2+) very low

input.anchor: Base rate: ~70-85% of signed FL death warrants proceed in DeSantis era. With only 1 scheduled execution, expected value anchors near 0.75-0.85.
input.assessment:
This is a discrete counting question with a very narrow distribution. Only one execution (Dusty Ray Spencer in Florida) is scheduled in the June 25-28 window. The core uncertainty is whether Spencer's execution proceeds or is stayed.

Arguments for proceeding (~85%): Circuit court denied postconviction claims; FL Supreme Court hasn't issued a stay despite pending appeal; DeSantis-era FL very aggressive (10 warrants signed in 2026, ~8-9 executions carried out); execution marked 'serious' by independent tracker; 34+ years of appellate history largely exhausted.

Arguments for stay (~15%): FL Supreme Court appeal SC2026-0880 still open; novel arguments about age (74, would be oldest FL execution ever) and allegedly expired drugs; Florida Supreme Court did stay the Duckett execution in March 2026 (though that was a DNA evidence case, very different).

P(2+) is negligible (~2%) since no other executions are scheduled and emergency scheduling doesn't happen in 4-day windows.

Distribution: P(0)=0.148, P(1)=0.838, P(2)=0.013, P(3+)=0.002. Expected value ~0.87.
input.calibration_notes: Distribution is intentionally tight because the question window is very short (4 days) and only 1 execution is on the schedule. The 15% stay probability is calibrated against FL's recent ~15-25% warrant-stay rate in the DeSantis era. The FL Supreme Court appeal is the main risk factor, but most death penalty appeals at this stage are denied in FL.
input.key_uncertainties: Main uncertainty: Will the FL Supreme Court grant a stay on Spencer's appeal (SC2026-0880)? The novel age/expired drugs arguments add some uncertainty but FL courts have generally rejected similar challenges. Secondary: Could any completely unscheduled execution materialize? (Extremely unlikely.)
input.tool_audit: Useful: research() provided detailed Spencer case status and FL execution history; web_search found current news about Spencer's legal challenges; fetch_url confirmed DPIC and SMU tracker both show only Spencer scheduled; Wikipedia confirmed 15 US executions in 2026 so far with several FL executions. All tools returned relevant results.
input.update_triggers: FL Supreme Court ruling on SC2026-0880 would be the key trigger. A stay would shift to P(0)≈90%+. A denial would shift to P(1)≈92%+. Any news of a surprise execution scheduling elsewhere would shift P(2) up.
input.process_reflection: This question is well-suited to the tooling. The discrete distribution is concentrated and straightforward. Research sub-agent provided good case-specific detail on Spencer's legal status. Direct web searches confirmed no other executions are scheduled. The main analytical challenge is estimating the stay probability, which requires judgment about FL Supreme Court behavior in the DeSantis era.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
## Verification Summary

**Factors checked against trace:**

1. **Factor 1** (Only one execution scheduled): ✅ Confirmed by DPIC fetch (only Spencer on June 25, next is Sochor July 14) and SMU tracker fetch (only Spencer listed for June 25-28).

2. **Factor 2** (Spencer execution likely to proceed): ✅ SMU tracker fetch confirms "marked as serious (*) - likely to proceed." Circuit court denial confirmed by fladeathpenalty substack fetch and research entry. The "~70-85% warrant completion rate" is supported by the historical research entry's estimate of "70-85% completion" for recent DeSantis-era warrants. Minor imprecision: "~8-9 FL executions already carried out in 2026" — the research says Spencer would be "approximately the 8th-9th," meaning 7-8 have already occurred, not 8-9. This doesn't materially affect the forecast.

3. **Factor 3** (FL Supreme Court appeal pending): ✅ Confirmed via FL Supreme Court docket reference (SC2026-0880, docketed June 9, status Open) and fladeathpenalty substack fetch. Age (74) and expired drugs arguments confirmed by Tampa Free Press source.

4. **Factor 4** (No other executions scheduled June 26-28): ✅ Confirmed by both DPIC fetch and SMU tracker fetch. Next execution is July 14.

**Double-counting**: Factors 1 and 4 both reference the absence of other scheduled executions, drawing on the same DPIC/SMU evidence. However, they serve distinct analytical purposes — Factor 1 establishes the mode at 1, while Factor 4 constrains the upper tail. The logit magnitudes are modest (+1.5 and -0.3) and the overlap doesn't distort the forecast.

**Wrong-direction factors**: All logit signs are consistent with factor descriptions.

**Resolution criteria**: Present and well-engaged. The agent correctly focuses on DPIC as the primary resolution source and counts only carried-out executions.

**Counterargument quality**: Substantive — identifies the open FL Supreme Court appeal as a real mechanism, references the Duckett stay precedent (correctly noting it was a DNA case), suggests the stay probability could be higher (20-25%), and mentions surprise scheduling tail risk. This is a genuine adversarial examination.

**Probability assessment**: The distribution P(0)=0.148, P(1)=0.838, P(2)=0.013, P(3+)=0.002 with center at 1.0 is well-calibrated. The ~85% probability of Spencer's execution proceeding is consistent with the 70-85% DeSantis-era base rate, adjusted upward by the circuit court denial, absence of a stay 8 days out, and "serious" tracker designation. The ~15% stay risk appropriately reflects the open FL Supreme Court appeal. I see no meaningful gap between my independent assessment and the agent's.

**Confidence (0.72)**: Appropriate — the evidence is specific and strong, but the binary uncertainty (does the FL Supreme Court act?) introduces genuine irreducible risk.

**Tool audit**: Agent's narrative matches actual metrics — no discrepancies found.
